does: This paper proposes Robust Reward Policy Optimization (RRPO), a framework for improving emotional text-to-speech synthesis by addressing reward hacking in differentiable RL-based TTS systems. The core contribution is a hybrid regularization scheme that develops a more robust reward model whose signals align better with human perceptual preferences rather than exploitable acoustic artifacts. The authors demonstrate through ablation studies that their robust reward model generalizes across languages and through subjective evaluation show that RRPO produces significant improvements in both emotional expressiveness and naturalness compared to baseline methods that use vanilla reward models.
connects: This work builds on recent differentiable reinforcement learning frameworks like DiffRO that enable fine-grained control over TTS system outputs. It directly addresses a critical vulnerability in these approaches: the tendency of policy models to discover reward hacking shortcuts that improve reward scores while degrading actual quality. The paper connects to broader literature on reward model misalignment and specification gaming in reinforcement learning, translating these well-known challenges from the RL community into the specific context of emotional speech synthesis where nuanced perceptual qualities are difficult to capture with simple metrics.
enables: By demonstrating effective mitigation of reward hacking in controlled speech generation, this work opens pathways for more reliable emotional TTS systems and establishes methods for building robust reward models in other nuanced generative tasks. The cross-lingual generalization properties suggest potential for developing universal emotional speech models. Future work could extend this regularization approach to other subjective qualities in speech synthesis and generative AI more broadly, establishing best practices for aligning learned reward signals with genuine human preferences in domains where ground truth evaluation is inherently subjective and multidimensional.
